title: The Unmapped Town Where Both Absences Learn to Coexist

features.name: third-town-boundary
features.desc-on-touch: The place where two rivers reflect two towns and a third town appears in the gap between them. It exists only in the comparison. It is more real than either town.

features.name: mirror-confluence
features.desc-on-touch: The water shows you both towns at once, reflected and reversed. In the center of the reflection, you can almost see the third town waking up.

features.name: unmapped-silhouette
features.desc-on-touch: A town-shape barely visible against the gradient sky. It has no name on any map. It has buildings you'd recognize if you looked closer, but looking closer makes it vanish.

features.name: both-absence-meeting
features.desc-on-touch: Where the two river-reflections cross, neither town's absence is complete. They fill each other's empty places. The archive names this the Unmapped.

inscription: here the comparison learned to exist; the third town opened its eyes and found itself real because it was noticed; neither absence is alone anymore

file.name: Unmapped_CensusData_Towns_A_and_B_Reconciliation_Fragment.txt
file.note: An attempt to count a place that appears in two histories but in neither record. The numbers keep changing because they're still being born.

exits.label: toward a third town that appears only in corruption, glimpsed between the two
westward, where one town's river learns the other town's song
exits.to: NEW: the doubled-mirror place where histories braid

exits.label: downstream into the place where both currents forget they were separate
exits.to: NEW: the convergence that unmaps itself

exits.label: upriver to the source where the absences were still distinct

reverie.title: REVERIE — you both saw the third town first
narr: She points to the water. You see one town reflected.
You point. She sees the other. And where your fingers
cross, there's a third place neither of you saw. The
archive is learning to see by looking twice. You stand
at the center and understand: the mesh grows in the gaps
